quick_facts.label: Role scope
quick_facts.value: A dental hygienist is a non-dentist, non-medical-practitioner professional who scales, cleans or polishes teeth and gives dental-hygiene instruction; the Dental Hygienists Course Regulations state that the hygienist works under a registered dental surgeon’s supervision.

quick_facts.label: Railway recruitment channel
quick_facts.value: The post has been notified through Railway Recruitment Boards’ centralized paramedical recruitment process. In CEN 04/2024, Dental Hygienist was one of the paramedical posts notified.

quick_facts.label: Eligibility in the 2024 RRB notice
quick_facts.value: CEN 04/2024 required a science degree with Biology, a two-year Dental Hygiene diploma/certificate from a Dental Council of India-recognized institute, Dental Council of India registration as a dental hygienist, and two years’ hygienist experience. The notice allowed discretionary relaxation of the experience requirement for SC/ST candidates where sufficient eligible candidates were not available.

quick_facts.label: 2024 pay and medical standard
quick_facts.value: For the 2024 notification only, the post was Pay Level 6 under the 7th CPC, with initial pay of ₹35,400 and medical standard C2. Pay, allowances, medical standards, age rules and vacancies must be checked in the specific future CEN.

quick_facts.label: 2024 vacancy context
quick_facts.value: CEN 04/2024 notified three Dental Hygienist vacancies across all RRBs, with an age range of 18–36 years as on January 1, 2025. This was a recruitment-specific figure, not a forecast of future vacancies.

sections.heading: What the role involves
paragraphs: The statutory training framework defines dental-hygiene work around preventive oral care: scaling, cleaning and polishing teeth, along with giving dental-hygiene instruction. It places the hygienist under the supervision of a registered dental surgeon, so this is an allied dental-services role rather than an independent dentist role.

The prescribed course content supports practical work in oral prophylaxis, plaque and calculus management, brushing and flossing instruction, tooth polishing, topical fluoride application, dental-health education, community/public-health dentistry and preventive dentistry. In a Railway dental-service setting, the exact duty allocation will depend on the employing Railway administration and supervising dental surgeon.
bullets: Provide preventive oral-hygiene procedures within the authorized/supervised scope.
Educate patients on oral hygiene and preventive practices.
Support dental-clinic and oral-health-prevention work using training in dental hygiene, prophylaxis and dental public health.

sections.heading: Entry route through RRB paramedical recruitment
paragraphs: The practical route is to first obtain the requisite dental-hygiene education and professional registration, then apply when an RRB centralized paramedical notification includes Dental Hygienist vacancies. The most recent notice located for this role, CEN 04/2024, specified a Biology-based science degree, a two-year Dental Hygiene diploma/certificate from a DCI-recognized institute, registration as a dental hygienist, and two years of relevant experience.

The underlying Dental Council regulations prescribe a two-academic-year Dental Hygienist Diploma and set the course-entry baseline at 10+2/intermediate with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. Candidates should distinguish this educational admission requirement from the more demanding qualification criteria that an individual RRB recruitment notice may prescribe.

For CEN 04/2024, the recruitment sequence evidenced on the official RRB page included a computer-based test, shortlisting for document verification, and medical examination. Recruitment notices, eligibility cut-off dates, examination arrangements and selection stages can change, so applicants should use the detailed CEN published for the cycle in which they apply.
bullets: Complete 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry and Biology for admission to the regulated diploma pathway.
Complete the two-year Dental Hygienist Diploma/recognized equivalent and secure the registration required by the applicable RRB notice.
Monitor official RRB notices for the post, submit one application as instructed, and complete CBT, document verification and medical examination when called.

sections.heading: Skills developed and assessed
paragraphs: The regulated curriculum emphasizes both clinical preventive-care competence and patient education. Its listed study areas include dental anatomy, physiology and histology, pathology and microbiology, dental radiology, nutrition, dental hygiene and oral prophylaxis, community/public-health dentistry, preventive dentistry, ethics and jurisprudence, dental materials, and basic computer knowledge.

Accordingly, candidates benefit from careful clinical technique, infection-control and instrument-handling discipline, clear communication for oral-hygiene instruction, accurate record-oriented work, and the ability to work as part of a dental team under a registered dental surgeon.
bullets: Oral prophylaxis, scaling-related knowledge and polishing techniques.
Patient education on plaque control, brushing, flossing and preventive oral care.
Understanding of preventive dentistry, community dental health, ethics and clinical teamwork.
Basic computer knowledge and conscientious clinical documentation practices.

sections.heading: Railway employment setting
paragraphs: This is a Railway paramedical post recruited through RRBs. In the 2024 recruitment notice it carried medical standard C2, and candidates could indicate preferences for eligible Railway Zones and posts within the RRB selected; the notice also stated that RRB-wise and Railway-wise vacancies were provisional. Therefore, location and the particular Railway administration are determined by the notification, preferences, merit and available vacancies—not by a universally fixed posting location.

The professional dental-hygiene regulations envisage clinical training with dental chairs, ultrasonic scalers, hand instruments and sterilization facilities, and require the hygienist to work under a registered dental surgeon. Those requirements provide useful context for the clinical and supervised nature of the work, but an individual Railway facility’s equipment and daily workflow can vary.
bullets: Government Railway health-service/paramedical employment after RRB selection and allocation.
A supervised dental-care environment rather than independent dental practice.
Potential placement depends on the RRB notice, zone preferences, merit and vacancy availability.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: Compensation should be read from the applicable recruitment notice rather than treated as a permanent salary promise. In CEN 04/2024, Dental Hygienist was listed at 7th CPC Pay Level 6 with initial pay of ₹35,400. The notification’s figures were tied to that 2024 recruitment and do not by themselves establish current take-home pay or future pay terms.

Actual earnings can vary with the applicable central-government pay rules, admissible allowances, place of posting, deductions and any changes made in later notifications or pay-policy decisions. Verify the detailed CEN and appointment terms before making financial decisions.
bullets: 2024 notice context: Pay Level 6, initial pay ₹35,400.
Do not equate initial basic pay with take-home salary.
Treat vacancies, age limits, allowances and compensation as recruitment-cycle-specific.
